avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be warm. Moist and wet avalanches as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Even at night, temperatures were positive above 3000 m.
In particular above approximately 2500 m: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by sometimes favourable conditions, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.

As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural activity of medium moist and wet avalanches will rapidly increase, in particular on very steep sunny slopes. This also applies at the base of rock walls, including on very steep shady slopes at intermediate altitudes.
Especially on extremely steep slopes avalanches can release the wet old snow as well and reach large size in isolated cases.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded very early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften quickly. In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
As a consequence of mild temperatures and solar radiation the snowpack settled. These spring-like weather conditions will give rise to increasing consolidation of the snowpack.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will persist.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes above approximately 1800 m. In the regions exposed to a lot of new snow caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and. In addition the fresh wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are capable of being triggered in some locations. At intermediate altitudes the danger is a little lower. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: A lot of snow has fallen over a wide area. In addition sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. New snow and wind slabs are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes also at high altitude.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Rise of the zero-degree level up to 3400 m.

The early morning will see favourable conditions, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent from the late morning. These conditions will cause a rapid weakening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes. Medium-sized natural avalanches are possible here. Increase in danger of moist and wet snow slides in particular in steep rocky terrain.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded very early.

Especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes avalanches can be triggered in the weakly bonded old snow. This applies in particular in case of a large load.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften earlier than the day before.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack.

At elevated altitudes snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In general, snow conditions in many regions are still good and are in line with or above the seasonal average. At low altitude and on sunny slopes the snowpack will soften quickly.

tendency.highlights: On Thursday it will be warm.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in some places by individual winter sport participants.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a rapid increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach large size in isolated cases. This applies in all aspects in particular below approximately 2400 m, as well as on very steep west, south and east facing slopes between approximately 2400 and 2800 m.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. The snowpack will become increasingly wet all the way through. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on steep sunny slopes between approximately 2400 and 2800 m.

Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, as well as on steep sunny slopes in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The avalanche danger will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible in all altitude zones. More recent wind slabs are clearly recognisable. The wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads, in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in isolated cases in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
These spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects. The various wind slabs have bonded with each other and the old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase quickly. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in the old snowpack, this applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in some cases. They can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m, and on south facing slopes in high Alpine regions.
Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase. As the day progresses the likelihood of avalanches being released will increase especially on extremely steep sunny slopes. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, and on south facing slopes also at high altitude. Wet avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where previous releases have taken place. In some cases these are medium-sized and can be released easily also by a single winter sport participant.

Backcountry tours should be concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Hard wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was quite good over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack. On very steep sunny slopes and at the base of rock walls the snowpack will soften rapidly.

On sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning. Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a rapid increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach medium size. This applies in all aspects in particular below approximately 2600 m, also on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2600 m, as well as on steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

The old wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations.

On steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The avalanche danger will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet loose snow avalanches in all aspects.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. Moist and wet avalanches are possible during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Isolated whumpfing sounds are a clear indication. In very isolated cases avalanches can also penetrate deep layers and reach quite a large size. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where hard layers are lying on a weakly bonded old snowpack are especially precarious.
The weather will be mild over a wide area. A clear night will be followed in the early morning by quite favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet avalanches will increase later. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be a gradual increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. Medium-sized natural avalanches are possible in particular on very steep slopes. The avalanche prone locations for wet avalanches are to be found in particular on very steep sunny slopes in all altitude zones and in steep rocky terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. These conditions will cause a very rapid weakening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes above the tree line. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be mild. The prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The Avalanche Warning Service currently has only a small amount of information that has been collected in the field, so that the avalanche danger should be investigated especially thoroughly in the relevant locality.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering. As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural avalanche activity will gradually increase. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Gliding avalanches can also occur.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snowpack thickness varies depending on exposure and altitude; especially on shady slopes, eroded areas are interspersed with deposits. In many places, hard surface crusts are present, and near the ridges, there are evident cornices. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be sunny at times.

avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase especially on extremely steep east and west facing slopes. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate deep layers and reach medium size in isolated cases.
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack on steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases. As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Thus far the weather has been mild over a wide area. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack.

The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes only a little snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: In the vicinity of peaks a generally favourable avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small small avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of wet snow slides and avalanches being released will increase in all aspects in all altitude zones.
